item.title: Southeast Asia tempts upstream investors as energy security concerns mount
item.description: Indonesia and Malaysia offering attractive terms that underpin E&P resurgence

item.title: Petronas boosts upstream presence in Turkmenistan
item.description: Exploration and development deals signed in Ashgabat, wider collaboration on the cards

item.title: Hanwha Ocean on board proposed $15.7 billion Canada FLNG project
item.description: Kanata liquefied natural gas export facility touted with 12 million tpa capacity

item.title: Russia declares breakthrough in LNG compressor manufacturing capability
item.description: Moscow continues to develop two large LNG projects despite European Union plans to fully halt Russian gas imports by end of 2027

item.title: Adura resubmits emissions statements for UK's Rosebank and Jackdaw fields
item.description: Development consent process for Rosebank and Jackdaw fields has become a political lightning rod in recent years

item.title: Adura resubmits emissions statements for UK's contentious Rosebank and Jackdaw fields
item.description: Development consent process for Rosebank and Jackdaw fields has become a political lightning rod in recent years

item.title: Rising gas-fired power demand creating new market for carbon capture: Baker Hughes
item.description: Industry ‘needs’ gas turbine decarbonisation to manage emissions from power sector

item.title: Petrobras to contract tech solution for hydrogen production
item.description: Bidding opportunity aims to develop national technology and reduce costs

item.title: OMV remains bullish on Black Sea and Norway after exploration setbacks
item.description: OMV executive vice president for energy Berislav Gaso tells Upstream the company remains “very enthusiastic” about exploration in Black Sea, while it is re-evaluating how to proceed with exploration in Norway's Voring basin

item.title: CCS costs must drop by at least 30%, says Galp technology chief
item.description: Marco Ferraz tells Upstream panel that modularisation is key to reduce cost of capture

item.title: Iraqi Kurdistan field remains offline after US-Iran peace deal
item.description: Gulf Keystone Petroleum says it is working with stakeholders to secure safe restart

item.title: UK-listed independent facing loss of flagship offshore asset
item.description: Timor-Leste government moves to terminate Sunda Energy’s Chuditch PSC

item.title: Corcel targets critical onshore Angola well in 'dream' exploration play
item.description: Junior player's chief operations officer Richard Lane highlights Kwanza basin potential

item.title: $400 million investment approved for Norway's largest gas field
item.description: New wells and subsea equipment required to maintain high production at Troll

item.title: Socar to explore Saudi-based group's financing options for energy projects
item.description: Socar agrees MoU with Islamic Corporation for the Development of the Private Sector to consider suitable developments

item.title: Valeura Energy boosts Thai oil production
item.description: Increased flow rates after drilling and workover campaign at Nong Yao field

item.title: Excited new owner at Barents Sea field sees production lifting off
item.description: Orlen Upstream Norway has agreed to acquire 20% of Goliat oilfield

item.title: Chinese energy regulator urges higher investment in oil and gas infrastructure
item.description: Chinese regulator says oil and gas infrastructure built over past five years has strengthened country's ability to withstand geopolitical tensions

item.title: This week's leading oil and gas appointments
Chevron, ExxonMobil and Shell in this week's leading oil and gas appointments
Suriname receives first offer for deepwater block in bid round
item.description: Open-door offering will roll for two years until November 2027

item.title: US firm files arbitration against Brazilian player over deepwater field rights
item.description: Westlawn argues Ecopetrol move for control of Brava could trigger right of first refusal over Atlanta

item.title: Laden QatarEnergy LNG carrier heads through Strait of Hormuz in open view
item.description: Vessels in ballast muster outside Middle East Gulf following US-Iran deal poised for Qatari export restart

item.title: Namibia government relieves Shino of duties
item.description: Acting petroleum commissioner appointed by Ministry of Industries, Mines & Energy

item.title: Alison-Madueke acquitted of bribery
item.description: Jury in London court cleared former Nigerian petroleum minister of six charges of bribery

item.title: Petrobras evaluates gas giant Jupiter with ‘fresh set of eyes’
item.description: Brazilian company trying to cut redundancies to make project economically viable

item.title: 'Here to stay': Middle East upstream investment set to weather recent challenges
item.description: Close to $100 billion worth of greenfield projects are in the pipeline in the Middle East, while repair bills are already nearing $50 billion

item.title: Equinor eyes Angola exploration wells after 'deepening' portfolio
item.description: Norwegian player's head of exploration outlines drilling plans in Angola, Brazil and Argentina

item.title: German energy minister unwilling to let TotalEnergies return offshore wind sites
item.description: Katherina Reiche reportedly insists that bids entered are legally binding contracts as oil firms seek to give back several gigawatts of sites won in 2023-25

item.title: US-Iran peace deal reopens Strait of Hormuz and restarts Iranian oil sales
item.description: MoU between Iran and US sees quick oil sanctions relief but leaves future of Strait of Hormuz an open question
